title: ipomopsis longiflora var. neomexicana
intro: Ipomopsis longiflora var. neomexicana, also known as New Mexico gilia, is a perennial plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. The herbal preparations of Ipomopsis longiflora var. neomexicana often involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to make teas or taken as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
